general_feedback: The two filled-in @return descriptions are accurate, clear, and concise. The `compareTo` return doc faithfully describes the three-way sign convention (and matches the parallel doc on `compare` at line 77). The `orderingToOrdered` return doc correctly characterizes the result as an `Ordered[T]` view of `x` that delegates to `ord`, which precisely matches the implementation. Both follow Scaladoc conventions (lowercase start, backticks for code references) and are consistent in style with the surrounding documentation.
